content.verdict: The Fairmont Royal Palm Marrakech operates on a scale that most Marrakech hotels cannot match: 231 hectares of olive groves, a 10-acre lake, and an 18-hole Cabell Robinson championship golf course, all ringed by Atlas views on three sides. For honeymooners who want the security and polish of a global brand — breakfast that actually works on arrival day, a concierge team that can fix anything in two phone calls, and a golf course you can play without leaving the resort — this is the most reliable big-ticket option in the Palmeraie. It is not the most atmospheric address in Morocco (that title belongs to a riad or a kasbah) but it is the most comfortable, and for many couples that tradeoff is exactly right.
content.best_room: Book a Fairway Suite with terrace and direct golf-course view, or upgrade to a Royal Palm Villa with private pool if budget allows. The villas are set in their own walled gardens well away from the main building and come with a dedicated butler, a private pool, and a terrace long enough to host a proper dinner for two.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival, orientation, welcome dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fairmont car from the airport through the olive groves, welcome mint tea on your terrace, quick sunset dip, dinner at Al Ain on the lake with a view of the floodlit fairways.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Couples spa day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day at the Willow Stream spa: couples hammam with black soap and ghassoul clay, signature massage, long lunch at the pool grill, afternoon by the 2,000m² main pool with cabana service.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Golf for one, trail ride for the other
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning round on the Cabell Robinson 18 for the golfer while the other takes a Berber trail ride through the olive groves, lunch together at the clubhouse, afternoon by the pool, tapas dinner at Le Caravane.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Medina and Majorelle
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hotel car to the Medina for a guided walk through the souks, the Saadian Tombs and Bahia Palace, lunch at Nomad rooftop, afternoon at Jardin Majorelle and the YSL Museum, dinner back at the resort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Atlas foothills and Berber village lunch
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private 4x4 into the Atlas foothills, stop at a Berber village cooperative for argan oil, long mountain lunch with a view, return by late afternoon, hammam before a candlelit tagine dinner in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lake day and stargazing dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow morning, paddleboat on the 10-acre lake, light lunch, Moroccan cooking class in the afternoon with the executive chef, private dinner set up on the lake jetty with lanterns and a sommelier pour.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final round and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the terrace, one last round of golf or a jog along the fairways, late check-out at 15:00, spa cool-down, and a private transfer back to Marrakech Menara airport.

caveats: The scale is enormous — 231 rooms across 231 hectares — and getting from your villa to the spa or restaurant is a buggy ride rather than a short walk; this reads as either an adventure or an inconvenience depending on your mood.
The hotel welcomes families and can host large conferences; during school holidays and golf weeks the main pool area gets busy, so honeymooners should ask for a room near the quieter adult zones.
The Palmeraie setting means a 25–35 minute drive each way to the Medina; if walking straight into the souks each morning matters to you, a Medina riad may suit better.

faqs.question: Is the golf course open to non-residents?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Al Maaden-adjacent Cabell Robinson course accepts outside green fees, but residents get priority tee times and preferential rates. Clubs can be rented at the pro shop and lessons are available.

faqs.question: Is Fairmont Royal Palm adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, it is family-friendly with a kids' club, but the estate is so large that honeymooners can easily find quiet corners. Request a room in the quieter wings at the time of booking.

faqs.question: How is the spa?
faqs.answer: Willow Stream Spa has 11 treatment rooms, a traditional hammam, vitality pool and a full menu of Moroccan rituals using argan, rose and ghassoul clay. Couples packages are available.

faqs.question: Can we arrange private dining?
faqs.answer: Yes. The team sets private dinners at the lake jetty, in the olive groves, by the main pool or on your terrace. Advance notice of 48 hours is ideal.

faqs.question: Is it far from the Medina?
faqs.answer: Approximately 25–35 minutes by hotel car depending on traffic. A hotel shuttle runs several times a day to Gueliz and the Medina during high season.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: Mid-March to early June and mid-September to mid-November are the sweet spots. The golf is most enjoyable in spring and autumn; summer temperatures can exceed 40°C.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Fairmont Royal Palm Marrakech

Dear Fairmont Royal Palm reservations team,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon and would like to request availability and your best honeymoon rate for 7 nights from [ARRIVAL DATE] to [DEPARTURE DATE] in a Fairway Suite or, if possible, a Royal Palm Villa with private pool.

We would also appreciate:
- A private airport transfer from Marrakech Menara (RAK)
- Breakfast included and any current honeymoon amenities
- A couples hammam ritual and a private lakeside dinner during our stay
- One 18-hole round of golf on the Cabell Robinson course
- Late check-out on departure day

Could you also confirm your deposit and cancellation terms? We are celebrating our marriage and any small gesture to mark the occasion would be very welcome.

Thank you and looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
[FULL NAMES]
[EMAIL] | [PHONE]
